# rubixclient

Go client for the Rubix API. One import, one constructor, access to every endpoint.

## Install

```bash
go get github.com/NubeIO/rubix-sdk@latest
```

## Quick Start

```go
package main

import (
    "fmt"
    "log"

    rubixclient "github.com/NubeIO/rubix-sdk/pkg/rubixclient"
)

func main() {
    c := rubixclient.New("http://localhost:9000")
    c.SetToken("my-api-key")

    // Use any auto-generated endpoint
    resp, err := c.Ping.Status("my-org", "my-device")
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }
    fmt.Println(resp)
}
```

## Structure

```
pkg/rubixclient/
  client.go              # RubixClient -- single entry point (manually maintained)
  client/                # Auto-generated from RAS spec (do not edit)
    client.go            #   Base HTTP client + options
    admin/               #   Per-resource sub-clients
    query/
    doc_assets/
    ...
  apps/                  # Extended client -- hand-written, high-level helpers
    apps.go
```

| Layer | Generated? | Purpose |
|-------|-----------|---------|
| `client/` | Yes (`make codegen`) | 1:1 mapping of every RAS endpoint |
| `apps/` | No | Typed helpers that compose raw endpoints |
| `client.go` | No | Wires everything into `RubixClient` |

## Auto-Generated Clients

Every resource defined in the Rubix RAS (Routing API Specification) gets a sub-client on `RubixClient`. These are generated by `make codegen` and should not be edited by hand.

```go
c := rubixclient.New("http://localhost:9000")

// Admin
c.Admin.DisableOrg(orgId, body)
c.Admin.EnableOrg(orgId, body)
c.Admin.GetOrgStatus(orgId)

// Auth
c.Auth.Signup(body)
c.Auth.Login(body)

// Nodes
c.Nodes.GetNode(orgId, deviceId, nodeId)
c.Nodes.CreateNode(orgId, deviceId, body)

// Query
c.Query.Create(orgId, deviceId, body)    // POST query
c.Query.Filter(orgId, deviceId, ...)     // GET query
c.Query.History(orgId, deviceId, body)
c.Query.Batch(orgId, deviceId, body)

// Devices, Orgs, Plugins, Flows, Pages, etc.
```

All generated methods follow the same signature pattern:

```go
func (rc *ResourceClient) ActionName(pathParams..., queryParams..., [body interface{}], opts ...client.Option) (interface{}, error)
```

### Options

Pass options to any generated call:

```go
import "github.com/NubeIO/rubix-sdk/pkg/rubixclient/client"

resp, err := c.Nodes.GetNode(orgId, deviceId, nodeId,
    client.WithHeader("X-Custom", "value"),
    client.WithQueryParam("extra", "param"),
    client.WithToken("override-token"),
)
```

## Extended Clients

Extended clients are hand-written wrappers that provide higher-level, typed methods on top of the generated endpoints. They are prefixed with `Extended` so they are easy to identify.

### ExtendedApps

Query and download apps/plugins from the Rubix app store.

```go
c := rubixclient.New("http://localhost:9000")
c.SetToken("my-api-key")

orgId := "my-org"
deviceId := "my-device"

// List all bios apps
apps, err := c.ExtendedApps.ListApps(orgId, deviceId, "")

// List apps for a specific platform
apps, err := c.ExtendedApps.ListApps(orgId, deviceId, "linux-arm64")

// List all plugins
plugins, err := c.ExtendedApps.ListPlugins(orgId, deviceId, "")

// List plugins for a specific platform
plugins, err := c.ExtendedApps.ListPlugins(orgId, deviceId, "linux-amd64")

// Get all versions of a specific app
versions, err := c.ExtendedApps.GetApp(orgId, deviceId, "rubix", "linux-arm64")

// Get a specific plugin by name
bacnet, err := c.ExtendedApps.GetPlugin(orgId, deviceId, "bacnet", "linux-arm64")

// Download an asset by node ID (from query results)
data, err := c.ExtendedApps.DownloadApp(orgId, deviceId, apps[0].ID)
```

#### AppAsset Fields

Each result from `ListApps`/`ListPlugins`/`GetApp`/`GetPlugin` returns typed `AppAsset` structs:

```go
type AppAsset struct {
    ID       string           // Node ID (use for download)
    Name     string           // Filename (e.g. "rubix-linux-arm64.tar.gz")
    Type     string           // Always "docs.asset"
    Settings AppAssetSettings // Platform, version, asset type, file format
    Parent   *AppAssetParent  // Parent folder/release info
}

type AppAssetSettings struct {
    Platform   string // "linux-arm64", "linux-amd64", "windows-amd64", "any"
    Version    string // Semver e.g. "2.5.0"
    AssetType  string // "bios-app", "plugin", "binary", "riot-package", "document"
    FileFormat string // "tar.gz", "zip", "bin", "pdf"
}
```

## Regenerating the Client

When the Rubix RAS spec changes, regenerate:

```bash
make codegen        # Regenerate client/ from RAS
make codegen-clean  # Remove generated code (before full regen)
```

The codegen reads the RAS YAML from the rubix repo path configured in `scripts/path.yaml`.

## Adding a New Extended Client

1. Create a new package under `pkg/rubixclient/` (e.g. `pkg/rubixclient/alerts/`)
2. Accept `*client.Client` in the constructor
3. Add a field to `RubixClient` in `client.go` prefixed with `Extended`
4. Wire it up in `New()`

```go
// In pkg/rubixclient/client.go

import "github.com/NubeIO/rubix-sdk/pkg/rubixclient/alerts"

type RubixClient struct {
    // ...existing fields...
    ExtendedAlerts *alerts.AlertsClient
}

func New(baseURL string) *RubixClient {
    c := client.NewClient(baseURL)
    return &RubixClient{
        // ...existing wiring...
        ExtendedAlerts: alerts.NewAlertsClient(c),
    }
}
```
